slides.title: Arrays vs Vectors ⚖️
slides.content: Arrays `[1,2,3]` are fast and fixed on the Stack. `Vec<T>` vectors live on the Heap and can grow dynamically.

slides.title: Creating Vectors 🚄
slides.content: Create an empty vector or use the `vec!` macro.
slides.codeSnippet:
let mut v1: Vec<i32> = Vec::new();
let mut v2 = vec![1, 2, 3];

quizData.question: What is the primary difference between Arrays and Vectors?

options.text: Arrays are fixed-size, Vectors grow dynamically

quizData.explanation: Vectors allocate dynamically on the heap.

slides.title: Reading Data 📖

quizData.question: Calling `v.get(99)` on a 3-item vector safely returns `None` instead of crashing the program.
quizData.explanation: The `.get()` method returns an Option, which is safer than direct bracket syntax.
